items.title: View every uploaded document fully offline
Welcome screen offers sign up and login
Vessel type — main categories and subcategories
Upload a certificate via file upload
Scan a certificate with the camera
Save disabled until a file is attached
iOS — subscription screen without dashboard flash
Android — subscription screen without dashboard flash
Price consistency — paywall vs Apple Pay sheet
Both offline — different records, no conflict

__m.purpose: Wave Baseline (Session 0) — catalog of all planned test cases. All Standby; clone flows into a build session to execute.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: several documents uploaded across different folders
2. Enable airplane mode (zero connectivity)
3. Force-quit the app, then cold-start it
4. Open each folder and view every uploaded document
f.expected: Every document opens and renders completely with zero connectivity, including after force-quit + cold start. STOP-SHIP if broken.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: a multi-page document uploaded to a chosen folder
2. Reopen the document from its folder
f.expected: Document is in the folder it was tagged to, all pages present, correct orientation, no corruption — exactly what was uploaded.

f.steps:
1. Enable airplane mode
2. Cold-start the app
3. Navigate: dashboard → folders → viewer → profile
f.expected: All primary navigation works offline; no crash, no infinite spinner, no dead screens.

f.steps:
1. Fresh install
2. Launch the app
f.expected: Welcome/Get Started screen shows with both Sign up and Log in options.

f.steps:
1. Tap Sign up
2. Enter email, password, first name, last name; pick nationality from dropdown
3. Submit
f.expected: Account created; a 6-digit verification code is emailed; verification screen shows.

f.steps: 1. Create an account leaving middle name empty
f.expected: Account creates without middle name; no validation error.

f.steps:
1. On sign up, enter 'not-an-email' as email
2. Submit
f.expected: Clear inline validation error; no code sent; no account created.

f.steps:
1. On sign up, leave email/password/first/last name empty in turn
2. Attempt submit each time
f.expected: Each required field flags a clear error; middle name never flags.

f.steps:
1. Pick a nationality during sign up
2. Complete account creation
3. Check profile
f.expected: Chosen nationality is saved and shown on the profile.

f.steps:
1. Complete sign up
2. Enter the emailed 6-digit code
f.expected: Verification succeeds; user proceeds to rank selection.

f.steps:
1. Complete sign up
2. Enter an incorrect 6-digit code
f.expected: Clear error; user can retry; no progress with a wrong code.

f.steps:
1. From Welcome, tap Log in
2. Enter valid email + password
f.expected: User lands in the app (dashboard/wallet).

f.steps: 1. Log in with a valid email but wrong password
f.expected: Clear, professional error message; no crash; no access.

f.steps:
1. From Log in, tap Forgot password
2. Enter account email
3. Follow the reset flow end to end
4. Sign in with the new password
f.expected: Reset completes; old password stops working; new password signs in.
f.notes2: spec §3 (details thin — see thinness report) · W-AUTH-011

f.steps:
1. From Welcome, choose Google login
2. Complete the Google flow with a test account
f.expected: Account created/signed in via Google; onboarding continues normally.

f.steps:
1. From Welcome, choose Apple login
2. Complete Sign in with Apple
f.expected: Account created/signed in via Apple; onboarding continues normally.

f.steps:
1. Start Google or Apple login
2. Cancel/dismiss the provider sheet mid-flow
f.expected: App returns to Welcome gracefully; no crash, no half-created account.

f.steps: 1. After verification, reach 'Choose your current rank'
f.expected: Main categories shown: Officer, Rating, Galley/Hotel Department, Staff Crew, Technical Support & Roles.

f.steps: 1. Select each main category in turn
f.expected: Subcategories match spec: Officer→Deck/Chief Officer; Rating→Engine/Motorman; Galley/Hotel→Cook/Chef/Messman; Staff Crew→DJ/Nanny/Photographer; Technical→IT Officer/Plumber.
f.notes2: spec §3 (full rank list MISSING from spec — see thinness report) · W-AUTH-016

f.steps:
1. After rank, reach 'Choose Your Vessel Type'
2. Open Commercial Cargo Ships
3. Open Tanker Ships
f.expected: Commercial Cargo lists its 12 subtypes (Container … Log Carrier); Tanker lists Oil Tanker (+ any others built).
f.notes2: spec §3 (vessel list CUT OFF in spec — see thinness report) · W-AUTH-017

f.steps: 1. Complete rank + vessel selection
f.expected: User reaches the app with a basic profile reflecting rank + vessel choices.
f.notes2: plan P1 (onboarding: rank & vessel, basic profile) · W-AUTH-018

f.steps:
1. During rank/vessel selection, force-kill the app
2. Reopen
f.expected: App resumes sensibly (back into onboarding or a defined restart point); no crash; no corrupt half-profile.

f.steps:
1. Open Certificates
2. Use the upload form to add a certificate file
3. Assign it to a category/folder
f.expected: Certificate uploads, appears in the chosen folder, opens in the viewer.

f.steps:
1. Open Certificates
2. Choose scan
3. Scan a physical document
4. Save to a folder
f.expected: Scan captures legibly and saves as a document in the chosen folder.
f.notes2: spec §3 (scan/upload) · NOTE: scanner integration is listed P2 in the plan — phase ambiguity, see thinness report · W-CERT-002

f.steps: 1. Add 3+ certificates to the same category
f.expected: All entries listed and individually openable; count correct.

f.steps: 1. Open an uploaded multi-page certificate
f.expected: Viewer shows all pages, supports zoom/scroll, renders text legibly.

f.steps:
1. Create/choose several categories
2. Move between folders
f.expected: Folder structure navigable; each doc is where it was tagged. (Folder create/rename/delete rules unspecified — see thinness report.)

f.steps:
1. Delete a certificate
2. Check the folder and total counts
f.expected: Certificate removed from folder; no orphan entries; viewer no longer opens it.
f.notes2: kit:offline-sync #4 (delete exists) · claude:edge for online case · W-CERT-006

f.steps:
1. Open a certificate
2. Use export/share
f.expected: Document exports (share sheet / file) intact and readable outside the app. (Formats unspecified — see thinness report.)

f.steps:
1. Open a certificate
2. Print via the OS print flow
f.expected: Print preview correct; full document prints.
f.notes2: spec §1 (print in case something gets lost) · W-CERT-008

f.steps: 1. Browse the certificate wallet home
f.expected: Presentation follows the Apple-Wallet-like card metaphor described in the spec (general check; per-screen Figma validation deferred).

f.steps:
1. Fresh account (no certs)
2. Open the Certificates tab
f.expected: 'No Certificates Yet' secure-wallet message; Add Certificate CTA; Search Certificates field; bottom nav = Certificates / Sea Service Records / Profile.

f.steps:
1. Add Certificate → fill ALL text fields
2. Attach NO file
3. Observe Save License button
f.expected: Correct behaviour defined: Save enabled with validation, OR intentionally disabled with a visible 'attach a file' hint. (Figma showed Save staying disabled with fields filled.)
f.notes2: figma:certificates edge (save gating) · POSSIBLE BUG · W-CERT-011

f.steps: 1. On populated My Certificates, review the category folders
f.expected: Five categories: Travel Documents, Licensing Certifications, Regulatory Statutory Documents, Industry Required, Other — colour-coded, each opening its own list.

f.steps: 1. Open a certificate card
f.expected: Full field set; Edit (pencil) + Delete (trash) icons; attached Files with View; a QR code.

f.steps: 1. On the detail screen, use the QR code / verification link
f.expected: In-app browser opens the verification URL showing 'Document status: VALID', with back/forward/reload. (Backend-dependent.)

f.steps: 1. Use Search Certificates with a full term, then a partial term
f.expected: Matching certificates surface; non-matches excluded; clear empty-result state.

f.steps:
1. Open Service Records
2. Add an entry (vessel, dates, role)
f.expected: Record saves and appears in the listing.

f.steps: 1. Add several records with different dates
f.expected: Listing shows all records, ordered sensibly (chronological).
f.notes2: plan P1 (listing) · plan P2 (chronological dashboard) · W-SR-002

f.steps:
1. Add a record spanning a known date range
2. Check the sea-days total
f.expected: Sea days match the date range exactly (inclusive rules consistent).

f.steps:
1. Add two records with overlapping date ranges
2. Check the total
f.expected: Total handles overlap per product rules without double-counting silently (verify + record actual behaviour).

f.steps: 1. Edit an existing record's dates
f.expected: Changes save; listing and sea-days totals update correctly.

f.steps: 1. Delete a record
f.expected: Record removed; totals update; no orphan data.

f.steps:
1. Open profile
2. Change rank and vessel type
3. Save
f.expected: Edits persist and reflect across the app.
f.notes2: plan P1 (profile management) · kit:offline-sync #5 (profile fields) · W-PROF-001

f.steps: 1. Open Contact us from settings
f.expected: Page loads and the contact action works (form/email).

f.steps: 1. On an account with NO active subscription, open Terms, Privacy, Contact Us
f.expected: All accessible without hitting the paywall.

f.steps:
1. Settings → delete account
2. Confirm
3. Attempt to sign in again
f.expected: Account deletes with clear confirmation; sign-in afterwards fails appropriately.

f.steps:
Precondition (one-time setup): sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app installed via TestFlight (sandbox billing).
1. Sign up as a new user, complete onboarding
2. On the subscription screen, tap "Start 30 days trial"
3. Confirm the purchase in the Apple payment sheet
f.expected: Payment sheet shows "Free Trial", purchase succeeds, app navigates to dashboard
f.notes2: kit:subscriptions iOS TC-1 · sandbox: 1mo≈5min, 12 renewals max · W-SUB-I01

f.steps:
Precondition (one-time setup): sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app installed via TestFlight (sandbox billing).
1. After completing TC-1, wait for the trial period to end (~5 min for monthly)
f.expected:
- Subscription auto-renews to paid. User stays on dashboard with full access
- Subscription status changes from trialing to active (In-Backend)

f.steps:
Precondition (one-time setup): sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app installed via TestFlight (sandbox billing).
1. Start a new trial (use a fresh sandbox account or clear purchase history)
2. Go to Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account > Manage > Subscriptions
3. Cancel the subscription
f.expected:
- User keeps access until trial end, then loses access
- After trial ends, app redirects to subscription paywall

f.steps:
Precondition (one-time setup): sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app installed via TestFlight (sandbox billing).
1. Subscribe (past the trial period)
2. Go to Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account > Manage > Subscriptions
3. Cancel the subscription
4. Wait for the current period to end (~5 min for monthly)
f.expected:
- User keeps access until period ends
- After expiry, app redirects to subscription paywall

f.steps:
Precondition (one-time setup): sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app installed via TestFlight (sandbox billing).
1. Subscribe and let it renew up to 12 times (takes ~60 min for monthly at fastest speed)
f.expected:
- After 12 renewals, subscription expires automatically
- App redirects to subscription paywall

f.steps:
Precondition (one-time setup): sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app installed via TestFlight (sandbox billing).
1. Subscribe on one sandbox account
2. Sign out of the app, sign back in (or reinstall)
3. On the subscription screen, tap "Restore Purchases"
f.expected: Previous subscription is restored, user gets access

f.steps:
Precondition (one-time setup): sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app installed via TestFlight (sandbox billing).
1. Subscribe to monthly
2. Go to Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account > Manage > Subscriptions
3. Change to yearly (or vice versa)
f.expected: Plan changes at next renewal.

f.steps:
Precondition (one-time setup): sandbox Apple account signed in (Settings > Developer > Sandbox Account); app installed via TestFlight (sandbox billing).
1. Sign up as a brand new user (fresh sandbox account)
2. Complete the full signup flow (name, email verify, onboarding)
f.expected: After onboarding, subscription screen appears directly — NO brief flash of the dashboard/certificates screen

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Gmail in License Testers; app from internal test track; pay with 'Test card, always approves'
2. Sign up as a new user, complete onboarding
3. Tap 'Start 30 days trial'
4. Select 'Test card, always approves', confirm
f.expected: Sheet shows 'Free Trial'; success; dashboard; profile shows trialing.
f.notes2: kit:subscriptions Android TC-1 · test: trial≈3min, 6 renewals max; unacknowledged purchases auto-refund at 3min · W-SUB-A01

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Gmail in License Testers; app from internal test track; pay with 'Test card, always approves'
2. Complete W-SUB-A01
3. Wait ~3 minutes
f.expected: Converts to paid; status trialing → active; access retained.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Gmail in License Testers; app from internal test track; pay with 'Test card, always approves'
2. Start a fresh trial
3. Play Store > Payments & subscriptions → cancel Wave
f.expected: Access until trial end (~3 min), then paywall.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Gmail in License Testers; app from internal test track; pay with 'Test card, always approves'
2. Subscribe past trial
3. Cancel in Play Store
4. Wait for period end (~5 min)
f.expected: Access until period end, then paywall.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Gmail in License Testers; app from internal test track; pay with 'Test card, always approves'
2. Subscribe; allow 6 renewals (~30 min)
f.expected: Expires after 6 renewals → paywall.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Gmail in License Testers; app from internal test track; pay with 'Test card, always approves'
2. Subscribe with approve card
3. Switch to 'Test card, always declines' (Play Billing Lab) or resubscribe with it
4. Wait for next renewal
f.expected: Grace period first (brief access), then expiry if payment keeps failing; app shows error overlay, never crashes.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Gmail in License Testers; app from internal test track; pay with 'Test card, always approves'
2. Subscribe
3. Sign out/in or reinstall
4. Tap 'Restore Purchases'
f.expected: Subscription restored; access granted.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Gmail in License Testers; app from internal test track; pay with 'Test card, always approves'
2. Brand-new user: full signup
f.expected: Subscription screen appears directly — NO dashboard flash.

f.steps: 1. With a trialing account, then an active one, use certificates + service records
f.expected: All gated features fully usable in both states.
f.notes2: plan P2 (subscription gating) · kit quick reference · W-SUB-G01

f.steps: 1. On the paywall, review layout
f.expected: Header 'Stay Contract-Ready. Advance Your Career.'; benefit bullets; Monthly $5/mo vs Yearly $50/yr (16% off, pre-selected); '30 Days Free' banner; 'Start 30 days trial' CTA; 'Recover Subscription'; Apple auto-renew legal + Manage subscriptions.

f.steps:
1. From paywall tap Start 30 days trial
2. Read the Apple Pay sheet amount
3. Compare to the paywall's $5 / $50
f.expected: Apple Pay sheet price matches the selected plan. (Figma showed the sheet reading '10 $' against a $50/yr paywall.)

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Device A + Device B on the same account, both online and fully synced
2. Device A: airplane mode
3. Device A: add a new Sea Service Record
4. Verify it appears locally on A
5. Device A back online
f.expected: Record appears on Device B within a few seconds.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Device A + Device B on the same account, both online and fully synced
2. Device A offline
3. Edit an existing Service Record (e.g. vessel name)
4. Device A back online
f.expected: Updated record syncs to Device B.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Device A + Device B on the same account, both online and fully synced
2. Device A offline
3. Delete a Service Record on A
4. Verify it disappears locally on A
5. Device A back online
f.expected: Record disappears from Device B.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Device A + Device B on the same account, both online and fully synced
2. Repeat scenarios 1–3 with Certificates (add, edit, delete) on Device A offline
3. Bring A back online after each
f.expected: Each certificate change syncs to Device B correctly.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Device A + Device B on the same account, both online and fully synced
2. Device A offline
3. Update profile (rank, vessel type, career goal)
4. Device A back online
f.expected: Device B shows the updated profile.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Device A + Device B on the same account, both online and fully synced
2. Both devices offline
3. A: edit Record X (vessel name)
4. B: edit Record Y (different record)
5. Bring both online
f.expected: Both changes appear on both devices.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Device A + Device B on the same account, both online and fully synced
2. Both devices offline
3. A: Record X vessel name → 'Ship Alpha'
4. B: Record X vessel name → 'Ship Beta'
5. Bring both online
f.expected: Both devices converge to the same value (last-write-wins).
f.notes2: kit:offline-sync #7 · conflict UX question in thinness report · W-SYNC-007

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Device A + Device B on the same account, both online and fully synced
2. Device A offline
3. Make multiple changes: add 2 records, edit 1, delete 1
4. Wait 5+ minutes
5. Device A back online
f.expected: All changes batch-sync to Device B.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Device A + Device B on the same account, both online and fully synced
2. Start adding a record on Device A
3. Toggle airplane mode on/off rapidly mid-operation
f.expected: No duplicate records, no data loss; Device B receives exactly one record.

f.steps:
1. Precondition: Device A + Device B on the same account, both online and fully synced
2. Device A offline
3. Add a Service Record
4. Force-kill the app, reopen (still offline)
5. Verify the record persists locally
6. Go online
f.expected: Record persisted offline and syncs to Device B.

f.steps:
1. Upload certificates with distinct names
2. Search by name/partial text
f.expected: Matching documents found; non-matches excluded.
f.notes2: plan P2 (document search) — spec detail thin · W-ADV-001

f.steps: 1. Scan a certificate carrying an issue date, expiry date and certificate number
f.expected: Issue date, expiration date and certificate number are extracted correctly into the entry's fields.
f.notes2: plan P2 (auto extraction) · accuracy is safety-relevant (wrong expiry = real-world risk) · W-ADV-002

f.steps:
1. Open the career dashboard with several records
2. Apply filters
f.expected: Chronological listing correct; filters narrow correctly.
f.notes2: plan P2 — spec detail thin · W-ADV-003

f.steps:
1. Set a rank goal
2. Check the goal progress bar
f.expected: Goal saved; progress bar reflects progress logic.
f.notes2: plan P2 (user goals) — progress rules unspecified, see thinness report · W-ADV-004

f.steps: 1. Export a report from the dashboard
f.expected: Report exports with correct data. (Format unspecified.)

f.steps:
1. Enable notifications when prompted
2. Trigger a notification event (per dev guidance)
f.expected: Notification arrives and deep-links sensibly.

f.steps:
1. Deny the notification permission
2. Use the app normally
f.expected: No crash, no nagging loop; notification-dependent features degrade gracefully.

f.steps:
1. Open the web admin
2. Log in with admin credentials
3. Try a non-admin account
f.expected: Admin signs in; non-admin is rejected.
f.notes2: plan P3 (admin auth) — NO spec/design yet, see thinness report · W-ADMIN-001

f.steps: 1. In admin, open the users table
f.expected: Registered users listed with sensible columns.

f.steps: 1. Open a user from the table
f.expected: Profile viewer shows that user's details.
